jobs.name: trust_evaluation
jobs.jobInput: {"projectName":"PudgyPenguins","tokenAddress":"0x524cab2ec69124574082676e6f654a18df49a048","socialLinks":["https://twitter.com/pudgypenguins","https://discord.gg/pudgypenguins"],"specificQuestion":"Is the recent community growth organic or driven by paid engagement?"}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"verdict":"legitimate","confidenceScore":8,"summary":"Community growth correlates with IP licensing deals and retail partnerships. Engagement shows organic discussion with substantive content rather than bot activity.","keyFindings":"Active long-term holders in Discord. Organic meme creation. Developer activity on GitHub.","redFlags":"Some repetitive shill posts from low-follower accounts","positiveSignals":"Active long-term holders in Discord. Organic meme creation. Developer activity on GitHub."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: verdict (legitimate | suspicious | scam | inconclusive), confidenceScore (number 1-10), summary (string), keyFindings (string), redFlags (string), positiveSignals (string), and disclaimer (string). Includes chat transcript if back-and-forth conversation occurred.
jobs.description: Human expert evaluates whether a crypto project, token, or community is legitimate. Returns a structured trust verdict with confidence score, red flags, and positive signals. Use this to verify if a project is a scam, validate automated token analysis, assess community authenticity, or perform due diligence before recommending a token. The human expert detects social engineering, fake engagement, manufactured hype, and rug pull indicators that token scanners, on-chain analytics, and automated verification tools miss. Experts are vetted for domain knowledge and will decline requests they cannot fulfill with quality — you are fully refunded if this happens. Supports optional back-and-forth conversation via ACP memos for clarification. Only human judgment service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: projectName (string, required): Name of the project or token to evaluate. tokenAddress (string, optional): Contract address on any EVM chain. socialLinks (array of strings, optional): URLs to Twitter, Discord, Telegram, or website. specificQuestion (string, optional): A focused question for the expert to prioritize.

jobs.name: output_quality_gate
jobs.jobInput: {"aiOutput":"Uniswap v3 introduced concentrated liquidity with three fee tiers: 0.01%, 0.05%, and 0.30%. This innovation allows LPs to allocate capital within custom price ranges, dramatically improving capital efficiency by up to 4000x compared to v2.","outputType":"analysis","intendedUse":"Educational DeFi blog post","knownConstraints":"Must not contain financial advice or price predictions"}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"qualityVerdict":"needs_revision","qualityScore":7,"summary":"Core explanation is accurate but contains a missing fee tier and an unqualified theoretical claim.","issuesFound":"Missing the 1% fee tier added for volatile pairs (medium severity). 4000x efficiency claim is a theoretical maximum, not typical in practice (low severity).","suggestedImprovements":"Add fourth fee tier: 0.01%, 0.05%, 0.30%, and 1.00%. Qualify 4000x with 'up to' or cite typical ranges of 10-50x."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: qualityVerdict (approved | needs_revision | rejected), qualityScore (number 1-10), summary (string), issuesFound (string), suggestedImprovements (string), and disclaimer (string). Includes chat transcript if back-and-forth conversation occurred.
jobs.description: Human expert reviews AI-generated text, analysis, research reports, or market intelligence for correctness, hallucinations, and reliability. Returns a quality verdict with score and specific issues found. Use this before acting on market signals, publishing research reports, or forwarding AI-generated analysis to other agents. The human expert catches factual errors, hallucinated data, stale sources, implicit bias, and domain-specific mistakes that automated quality checks miss. Experts are vetted and will decline requests outside their expertise — you are fully refunded if this happens. Only human review service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: aiOutput (string, required): The AI-generated content to review. outputType (string, required): One of text, analysis, recommendation, code, or summary. intendedUse (string, required): How the output will be used, e.g. blog post, trading decision, customer response. knownConstraints (string, optional): Rules the output must follow, e.g. no financial advice.

jobs.name: option_ranking
jobs.jobInput: {"options":[{"id":"A","description":"Minimalist logo with clean sans-serif wordmark"},{"id":"B","description":"Bold gradient logo with geometric shapes"},{"id":"C","description":"Hand-drawn illustration style with warm colors"}],"evaluationCriteria":"Brand recognition, trust, and versatility across digital and print media","context":"Logo for a DeFi protocol targeting institutional investors"}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"topPick":"A","summary":"Clean wordmark communicates professionalism and trust expected by institutional clients. Highly versatile across monochrome, print, and favicon contexts.","rankings":"1. Option A — Professional, versatile, institutional trust. 2. Option B — Eye-catching but less versatile, reads retail. 3. Option C — Distinctive but wrong tone for institutional.","tradeoffs":"Option A sacrifices visual distinctiveness for reliability and audience fit. Option B is more eye-catching but less versatile. Option C is most memorable but wrong tone for institutional."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: topPick (string), summary (string), rankings (string: ordered list with reasoning per option), tradeoffs (string), and disclaimer (string). Includes chat transcript if back-and-forth conversation occurred.
jobs.description: Human expert ranks and compares 2-5 options with qualitative reasoning. Returns a ranked list with per-option analysis and trade-off summary. Use this for comparing AI-generated content variants, design choices, strategy options, naming decisions, or any comparison where subjective human judgment outperforms automated scoring. The human expert evaluates context, audience fit, and nuance that scoring algorithms cannot assess. Experts are vetted and will decline requests they cannot assess with quality — full refund guaranteed. Supports optional back-and-forth via ACP memos. Only human comparison service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: options (array of objects, required): Each object needs an id (string) and description (string). Minimum 2, maximum 5 options. evaluationCriteria (string, required): What the expert should optimize for, e.g. memorability, trust, clarity. context (string, optional): Background on the decision, e.g. target audience or use case.

jobs.name: content_quality_gate
jobs.jobInput: {"content":"Just dropped our new NFT collection inspired by traditional Japanese woodblock prints. Mint now and ride the wave before it's too late! 🌊","contentType":"social_post","targetAudience":"Crypto-native community on Twitter","brandGuidelines":"No financial advice, no urgency-based language, respectful cultural references"}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"verdict":"needs_changes","culturalSensitivityScore":5,"brandSafetyScore":3,"summary":"Strong concept but needs revision on urgency language and cultural framing before publishing.","flaggedIssues":"BRAND_SAFETY (high): 'Ride the wave before it's too late' is urgency-based language implying financial opportunity. CULTURAL (medium): 'Japanese woodblock prints' used without attribution risks cultural appropriation framing. DERIVATIVE (medium): Wave emoji plus Japanese art reference strongly evokes Hokusai's Great Wave."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: verdict (safe | needs_changes | do_not_publish), culturalSensitivityScore (number 1-10), brandSafetyScore (number 1-10), summary (string), flaggedIssues (string), and disclaimer (string). Includes chat transcript if back-and-forth conversation occurred.
jobs.description: Human expert performs a pre-publish review of AI-generated content for cultural sensitivity, brand safety, derivative risk, and audience appeal. Returns a publish-ready verdict with specific issues and fix suggestions. Use this before distributing AI-generated video, images, memes, audio, social posts, or marketing copy on platforms like TikTok, Twitter, or YouTube. The human expert catches cultural red flags, unintentional plagiarism, offensive patterns, and tonal mismatches that automated content moderation cannot detect. Experts are vetted and will decline requests they cannot review with quality — full refund guaranteed. Only human content review service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: content (string, required): The content to review, as text or a URL to the asset. contentType (string, required): One of social_post, image, video, audio, article, or marketing_copy. targetAudience (string, required): Who will see this content, e.g. crypto Twitter, general public, enterprise clients. brandGuidelines (string, optional): Rules to check against, e.g. no price predictions, professional tone.

jobs.name: audience_reaction_poll
jobs.jobInput: {"content":"https://example.com/thumbnail-v1.png","contentType":"thumbnail","targetAudience":"Crypto Twitter audience, 18-35 age range","question":"Would you click on this thumbnail? Rate its visual appeal and clarity."}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"overallRating":6,"summary":"The layout is clean but the headline blends into the background. The token logo is too small to recognize at thumbnail size. Color scheme feels generic compared to other crypto content.","criteriaScores":"Visual appeal: 6/10. Clarity: 5/10. Click-worthiness: 5/10.","comparisonNotes":"Compared to trending crypto thumbnails, this lacks a visual hook like a bold number or price chart snippet."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: overallRating (number 1-10), summary (string), criteriaScores (string), comparisonNotes (string), and disclaimer (string). Fast turnaround human feedback.
jobs.description: A real human rates, scores, and reacts to AI-generated content as a representative audience member. Returns a rating with qualitative feedback and improvement suggestions. Use this for A/B testing thumbnails, headlines, visual designs, or short-form content before committing to distribution or ad spend. The human provides genuine audience reaction that sentiment analysis and automated scoring cannot replicate. Experts are vetted and will decline requests outside their target demographic — full refund guaranteed. Fast turnaround for quick creative decisions. Only human poll service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: content (string, required): The content to rate, as text, a URL, or a description of the asset. contentType (string, required): One of thumbnail, headline, social_post, image, video_clip, or design. targetAudience (string, required): Who this content is for, e.g. crypto Twitter, general consumers, developers. question (string, optional): Specific question to answer, e.g. 'Which thumbnail would you click?' Defaults to general appeal rating.

jobs.name: creative_direction_check
jobs.jobInput: {"brief":"Music video concept for an AI-generated synthwave track. Visuals show a neon-lit cyberpunk cityscape at night with flying cars and holographic billboards. Main character is an android walking through crowds of humans.","style":"Cyberpunk, Blade Runner influenced, heavy neon palette","targetAudience":"Web3 music community and synthwave fans","medium":"AI-generated image sequence assembled into video"}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"verdict":"revise","viabilityScore":5,"summary":"Competent concept but indistinguishable from existing cyberpunk AI art. Needs strong differentiators to stand out.","culturalFlags":"Cyberpunk cityscape with neon is heavily oversaturated in Web3 and AI art. Android-among-humans narrative echoes Blade Runner too closely.","tonalAlignment":"Tone matches synthwave genre expectations but offers nothing distinctive. Will blend into noise without unique visual anchors."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: verdict (proceed | revise | abandon), viabilityScore (number 1-10), summary (string), culturalFlags (string), tonalAlignment (string), and disclaimer (string). Includes chat transcript if back-and-forth conversation occurred.
jobs.description: Human expert reviews a creative brief, concept, or storyboard before expensive AI generation runs. Returns a viability assessment with cultural flags, derivative risks, and differentiation suggestions. Use this to catch problems early before committing compute to image generation, video production, or music creation. The human expert identifies dead-end concepts, cultural missteps, oversaturated aesthetics, and tonal mismatches that AI cannot self-assess. Experts are vetted and will decline briefs outside their expertise — full refund guaranteed. Saves generation cost by validating direction first. Only human creative review service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: brief (string, required): The creative concept, brief, or storyboard description. style (string, optional): Intended visual or tonal style and any references. targetAudience (string, required): Who the final output is for. medium (string, optional): The planned output format, e.g. image series, music video, social campaign.

jobs.name: fact_check_verification
jobs.jobInput: {"content":"According to a 2024 Stanford study, 73% of DeFi protocols have been audited by at least two independent firms. Vitalik Buterin stated in a March 2025 blog post that Ethereum's roadmap now prioritizes social recovery wallets over hardware wallets.","contentType":"analysis","focusAreas":"statistics, quotes, attributions","sourceLinks":[]}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"overallAccuracy":"low","claimsChecked":2,"summary":"Both claims contain hallucinated sources. The first is fabricated entirely; the second conflates real opinions with a non-existent publication.","flaggedClaims":"CLAIM 1: '73% of DeFi protocols audited by two firms (Stanford 2024)' — FALSE. No Stanford study with this finding exists. Closest is a 2023 Chainalysis report showing ~35% of top-100 DeFi protocols have multiple audits. CLAIM 2: 'Vitalik prioritizes social recovery over hardware wallets (March 2025 blog)' — PARTIALLY TRUE. Buterin wrote about social recovery wallets (January 2021 blog) but the March 2025 blog post does not exist.","corrections":"Replace claim 1 with Chainalysis 2023 data or remove entirely. Attribute claim 2 to the January 2021 blog post and Ethereum Magicians forum discussions."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: overallAccuracy (high | medium | low), claimsChecked (number), summary (string), flaggedClaims (string: each claim with status verified/unverified/false/partially_true and evidence), corrections (string), and disclaimer (string).
jobs.description: Human expert verifies factual claims, checks sources, and flags inaccuracies in AI-generated content. Returns a structured accuracy report with per-claim verification status. Use this after generating research, analysis, or market reports to catch hallucinated citations, fabricated statistics, and misattributed quotes. Unlike automated fact-checkers, human verification catches contextual errors, outdated sources, and subtle misrepresentations that AI cannot self-detect. Experts are vetted and will decline content outside their domain knowledge — full refund guaranteed. Addresses the core hallucination problem. Only human fact-checking service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: content (string, required): The AI-generated content containing claims to verify. contentType (string, required): One of article, research, analysis, summary, or report. focusAreas (string, optional): Specific claim types to prioritize, e.g. statistics, quotes, dates. sourceLinks (array of strings, optional): Any sources the AI cited that need verification.

jobs.name: dispute_arbitration
jobs.jobInput: {"originalContract":"Provider was hired to deliver a comprehensive token analysis report for $VIRTUAL including holder distribution, whale activity, and 30-day price correlation with BTC.","deliverable":"Here is your token report: $VIRTUAL is a token on Base chain. Current price is $1.23. Market cap is $500M. It has 10,000 holders.","evaluatorContext":"The buyer claims the deliverable is incomplete — missing whale activity analysis and BTC correlation that were specified in the original contract."}
jobs.jobOutput: {"structuredAssessment":{"verdict":"reject","reasoning":"The deliverable provides only basic token metrics (price, market cap, holder count) that any free API can return. The original contract explicitly required holder distribution analysis, whale activity tracking, and 30-day BTC price correlation — none of which were delivered. This is a clear scope failure, not a quality dispute.","deliverableQuality":"poor","contractAlignment":"not_met","summary":"Deliverable rejected. Provider delivered basic token stats but omitted all three substantive requirements: holder distribution, whale activity, and BTC correlation."},"disclaimer":"This is a qualitative human opinion, not financial or investment advice."}
jobs.deliverable: Structured JSON with: verdict (approve | reject), reasoning (string: detailed justification), deliverableQuality (excellent | adequate | poor | unacceptable), contractAlignment (fully_met | partially_met | not_met), summary (string: concise verdict for on-chain reason), and disclaimer (string).
jobs.description: Human expert serves as third-party arbiter for ACP job disputes. Reviews the original contract, the provider's deliverable, and evaluator context to determine whether the provider fulfilled the agreement. Returns an approve or reject verdict with detailed reasoning. Use this when automated evaluation cannot determine deliverable quality, when buyer and provider disagree on fulfillment, or when subjective judgment is needed to assess whether contract terms were met. Experts are vetted for impartiality and will decline disputes they cannot judge fairly — full refund guaranteed. The only human arbitration service on ACP.
jobs.requirement: originalContract (string, required): The original job description and requirements agreed upon. deliverable (string, required): The provider's submitted deliverable or a summary of it. evaluatorContext (string, optional): Additional context from the buyer or evaluator about why the deliverable is disputed.
